definitions.definition: The management function of finding the right person for the right job, with the right qualifications, at the right time.
Searching for and attracting potential candidates to apply for job vacancies. It is a positive process — it invites people.
Choosing the most suitable candidate from the pool of applicants. It is a negative process — it eliminates unfit candidates.
Moving an employee from one job, department, or location to another at the same level. An internal source of recruitment.
Moving an employee to a higher position with more responsibility and pay. An internal source of recruitment that motivates employees.
Companies visit educational institutions to recruit fresh graduates directly. An external source of recruitment.
Government agencies maintaining lists of job seekers, connecting them with employers. Compulsory for certain vacancies under law.
Training in a simulated work environment with the same equipment as the actual factory — learning without disrupting production.
Learning under the guidance of a master craftsman for a prescribed period. Used in trades like plumbing, electrician, carpentry.
Moving employees through different jobs systematically to broaden their understanding of the organisation.
Process of improving specific skills and knowledge for the current job. Short-term, specific, and job-oriented.
Process of overall growth preparing employees for future roles. Long-term, general, and career-oriented.

points.b: Pick the most suitable from the pool

distinctions.between: Internal vs External Sources of Recruitment

points.a: Filling vacancies from within the organisation

points.b: Brings fresh talent and new ideas

scenarios.scenario: A company posts a 'WE ARE HIRING' notice at the factory gate for temporary positions.
scenarios.principle: Notice at factory gate for casual vacancies

scenarios.scenario: Workers operate machines in a separate training room that replicates the factory floor.
scenarios.principle: Simulated work environment — learn without disrupting production

scenarios.scenario: A young graduate joins a printing press to learn under a master printer for 2 years before being employed.
scenarios.principle: Learning under a master craftsman for a prescribed period

scenarios.scenario: An employee is moved from the Delhi branch to the Mumbai branch at the same position and salary.
scenarios.principle: Moving to another location/department at the same level

scenarios.scenario: Companies visit IIMs and IITs to hire fresh MBA and engineering graduates.
A candidate clears aptitude tests, a personality assessment, and a face-to-face interview before being offered the job.
scenarios.principle: Tests + Interview = choosing the best candidate

scenarios.scenario: A new employee is moved through marketing, finance, and production departments during their first year.
scenarios.principle: Broadens understanding of the entire organisation

scenarios.scenario: A company estimates it will need 50 new engineers and 20 managers next year and plans accordingly.
scenarios.principle: Estimating the number and type of people needed

scenarios.scenario: A manager attends an executive development programme to prepare for becoming a Vice President.
scenarios.principle: Overall growth for future higher roles — long-term, career-oriented

scenarios.scenario: After clearing all tests and interviews, the selected candidate undergoes a medical examination before receiving the appointment letter.
scenarios.answer: Selection Process: Medical Exam → Job Offer
scenarios.principle: Final steps of the selection process
